task_completion.visual_evidence: In the NextGen Connect Administrator (Channels view or Dashboard) or via API response, a channel named 'Latency_Monitor' is visible.

task_completion.description: Source connector is configured to listen on TCP port 6661
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Source tab shows 'TCP Listener' selected with the Port field set to '6661', or equivalent API configuration is visible.

task_completion.description: Logic is configured to calculate latency between MSH-7 and EVN-2 in minutes
task_completion.visual_evidence: JavaScript code or rules in a Filter, Transformer, or Destination settings that extracts MSH-7 and EVN-2, parses them using 'yyyyMMddHHmmss' format, and calculates the difference in minutes.

task_completion.description: Destination configured to route high latency (> 10 mins) messages to /home/ga/latency_high/
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Destination (e.g., File Writer) is configured with the Directory set to '/home/ga/latency_high/'. There should be evidence (like a filter or router rule) linking this to the > 10 minutes condition.

task_completion.description: Destination configured to route normal latency (<= 10 mins) messages to /home/ga/latency_normal/
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Destination (e.g., File Writer) is configured with the Directory set to '/home/ga/latency_normal/'. There should be evidence linking this to the <= 10 minutes condition.

task_completion.description: The channel is deployed and running
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Dashboard shows the 'Latency_Monitor' channel with a status of 'Started' or 'Deployed', or an API command shows successful deployment.

integrity.description: Agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI or REST API to configure the channel
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual NextGen Connect Administrator interface or terminal commands interacting with the Mirth REST API, not just a text editor with mock configurations.

integrity.description: The latency calculation logic is genuinely implemented
integrity.visual_evidence: The JavaScript code or routing rules actually perform the date parsing and math for MSH-7 and EVN-2, rather than just hardcoding paths or skipping the calculation.
The channel appears in the active Dashboard view with a 'Started' status, proving it was deployed and not just saved as a draft.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires a NextGen Connect (Mirth Connect) channel named 'Latency_Monitor'. Source: TCP Listener on port 6661. Routing logic must calculate latency = (MSH-7) - (EVN-2) in minutes, using 'yyyyMMddHHmmss' format. Messages with latency > 10 minutes go to a File Writer destination at '/home/ga/latency_high/'. Messages with latency <= 10 minutes go to a File Writer destination at '/home/ga/latency_normal/'. The channel must be deployed and started.
